info.title: Vex Protocol API
info.description: REST API for the Vex encrypted chat platform. Messages are serialized using msgpack.

owner.description: Server ID that owns this emoji

post.description: Authenticate with username and password credentials. Returns a JWT token and the authenticated user profile.
Initiate device-key authentication by submitting a deviceID and signing key. Returns a challenge nonce to sign.
Submit the signed challenge to complete device-key authentication. Returns a JWT token and the authenticated user profile.
Create a new user account with cryptographic device registration. Requires a signed registration payload with Ed25519 keys.
Return the current authenticated session including token expiry and user profile.
Invalidate the current JWT token, ending the session.

get.description: Request a scoped, time-limited action token for operations like registration, file upload, or device pairing.
Retrieve the public profile for a user by their ID.
List all registered devices for the given user, including signing keys and login timestamps.
List all chat servers the given user is a member of.
List all permission grants for the given user across all resources.
Retrieve details for a chat server by its ID.

post.description: Create a new chat server. The path parameter is used as the server name.

delete.description: Permanently delete a chat server. Requires owner-level permissions.

get.description: List all channels belonging to the given server.

post.description: Create a new channel within the given server.

get.description: Retrieve details for a channel by its ID.

delete.description: Permanently delete a channel. Requires sufficient permissions on the parent server.

get.description: Retrieve details for a server invite, including expiration and target server.

patch.description: Accept a server invite, granting the authenticated user membership and returning the new permission grant.

get.description: Returns server and database readiness status. Used by load balancers and monitoring.
